evals.prompt: How should I structure custom exceptions in a FastAPI service with multiple domains (users, posts, payments)?
evals.expected_output: Define a base AppError class in src/core/exceptions.py with status_code, detail, and code class attributes. Then subclass NotFoundError, ConflictError, UnauthorizedError etc. Each domain creates its own exceptions.py (UserNotFound, PostNotFound) that extends the core base classes. Register a single global exception_handler for AppError in main.py.

evals.prompt: My repository gets an IntegrityError from SQLAlchemy when a duplicate user is inserted. How should I handle this?
evals.expected_output: Catch IntegrityError in the repository method and raise a domain-specific ConflictError (e.g. UserAlreadyExists). Never let raw SQLAlchemy errors propagate to the service or router layers — they leak implementation details. Repository should raise business exceptions, not HTTP exceptions.

evals.prompt: Should I use try/except in every FastAPI route function to return different HTTP status codes?
evals.expected_output: No. Register a single global exception handler via app.add_exception_handler(AppError, handler). Business exceptions automatically map to HTTP status codes through their status_code class attribute. Route functions should not contain try/except — let exceptions propagate to the global handler.

should_not_trigger: How do I configure structured JSON logging in Python?
